title: Quiz 10
subtitle: B1-B2 Strategy Challenge
source: Original B1-B2 quiz created for this system

questions.question: By the time the fire alarm stopped, all the students _____ the building.
questions.explanation: Use past perfect for an action completed before another past moment.

questions.question: Ella was tired because she _____ the violin all afternoon.
questions.explanation: Past perfect continuous shows an activity continuing before a past result.

questions.skill: Duration up to a Future Time
questions.question: By July, Marco _____ English for two years.
questions.explanation: Future perfect continuous uses will have been + verb-ing to show duration up to a future time.

questions.question: If I had saved my pocket money, I _____ enough to buy the skateboard now.
questions.explanation: This mixed conditional connects a past action with a present result.

questions.question: If they had not missed the bus, they _____ late for the concert.
questions.explanation: Third conditional result uses would not have + past participle.

questions.question: I wish I _____ to your advice before the test.
questions.explanation: Use wish + past perfect for regrets about the past.

questions.question: I wish the neighbours _____ making so much noise at night.
questions.explanation: Wish + would is used for annoying habits or situations we want to change.

questions.question: The missing painting is believed _____ hidden in a private collection.
questions.explanation: A formal passive reporting pattern is be believed to be.

questions.question: The student _____ backpack was missing looked worried.
questions.explanation: Whose shows possession in a relative clause.

questions.question: The bridge was repaired last year, _____ made the journey much safer.
questions.explanation: Which can refer to the whole previous clause in a non-defining relative clause.

questions.question: _____ in simple language, the guide was easy for children to understand.
questions.explanation: A past participle clause can describe something in a shorter way: written in simple language.

questions.question: _____ the homework, Leo went outside to play basketball.
questions.explanation: Having + past participle shows that one action happened before another.

questions.question: Despite the fact that the route was long, the hikers _____ cheerful.
questions.explanation: Despite the fact that can introduce a full clause. The main clause still needs a verb.

questions.question: You can borrow the tablet _____ you return it before dinner.
questions.explanation: Provided that means only if.

questions.question: You may join the workshop _____ you wear safety gloves.
questions.explanation: On condition that means only if a condition is met.

questions.question: Mia prefers working alone, _____ Sam enjoys group projects.
questions.explanation: Whereas compares two different facts or preferences.

questions.question: You can choose _____ book you like from this shelf.
questions.explanation: Whichever means any one from a limited set of choices.

questions.question: She _____ forgotten the meeting; I reminded her twice this morning.
questions.explanation: Can't have + past participle shows that we think something was impossible in the past.

questions.question: You _____ the document. I already had a copy.
questions.explanation: Needn't have + past participle means someone did something, but it was not necessary.

questions.question: I cannot _____ that loud music while I am studying.
questions.explanation: Put up with means tolerate something annoying.

questions.question: The dentist told me to _____ sugary drinks.
questions.explanation: Cut down on means reduce the amount of something.

questions.question: I finally got round to _____ my desk.
questions.explanation: Get round to is followed by verb-ing and means finally do something after delaying it.

questions.question: The students will _____ an experiment to test their idea.
questions.explanation: Carry out means perform or complete a task, experiment, or plan.

questions.question: There is a strong _____ that the match will be cancelled. (probable)
questions.explanation: Probable is an adjective. Probability is the noun.

questions.question: Winning the award was a huge _____ for the whole team. (achieve)
questions.explanation: Achieve is a verb. Achievement is the noun.

questions.question: Regular practice can _____ your writing skills. (strong)
questions.explanation: Strong is an adjective. Strengthen is the verb meaning make stronger.

questions.question: The campaign raised _____ of plastic pollution. (aware)
questions.explanation: Aware is an adjective. Awareness is the noun.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: Had I known about the delay I would have waited at home
questions.explanation: Had I known is a formal alternative to If I had known.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: The report must be handed in by Friday
questions.explanation: Passive with a modal uses modal + be + past participle.

questions.reading: Nina's group built a water filter for the science fair. On the morning of the fair, the main tube cracked, and the group nearly gave up. Nina suggested using a clear bottle from the recycling box. The new filter did not look elegant, but it worked. The judges liked how the group explained both the problem and the solution, and Nina learned that prototypes are supposed to change.
questions.question: What went wrong on the morning of the fair?
questions.explanation: The main tube cracked.

questions.reading: Nina's group built a water filter for the science fair. On the morning of the fair, the main tube cracked, and the group nearly gave up. Nina suggested using a clear bottle from the recycling box. The new filter did not look elegant, but it worked. The judges liked how the group explained both the problem and the solution, and Nina learned that prototypes are supposed to change.
questions.question: Why did the judges like the project?
questions.explanation: They liked how the group explained the problem and the solution.
options: The group hid the broken tube

questions.reading: Nina's group built a water filter for the science fair. On the morning of the fair, the main tube cracked, and the group nearly gave up. Nina suggested using a clear bottle from the recycling box. The new filter did not look elegant, but it worked. The judges liked how the group explained both the problem and the solution, and Nina learned that prototypes are supposed to change.
questions.question: What did Nina probably learn?
questions.explanation: She learned that changing and improving a prototype is part of the process.
options: Problems can lead to better solutions
Science fairs are only about winning

questions.question: _____ surprised me was how calmly Ava solved the problem.
questions.explanation: A what-clause can be used for emphasis: What surprised me was...

questions.question: It was Sam _____ found the missing tickets.
questions.explanation: Cleft sentences use It was + person + who to emphasize the person.

questions.question: The teacher asked how long I _____ English.
questions.explanation: Reported questions use statement word order. A past reporting verb often moves the tense back.

questions.question: If you run across the wet floor, you risk _____ over.
questions.explanation: Risk is followed by verb-ing.

questions.question: We left early to avoid _____ stuck in traffic.
questions.explanation: Avoid is followed by verb-ing. Being stuck is a passive -ing form.

questions.question: The documentary is worth _____ because it explains the topic clearly.
questions.explanation: Worth is followed by verb-ing.

questions.question: After weeks of practice, Mina spoke with more _____. (confident)
questions.explanation: Confident is an adjective. Confidence is the noun.

questions.question: The chair was so hard that it felt _____. (comfort)
questions.explanation: Comfort is a noun or verb. Uncomfortable is the adjective meaning not comfortable.

questions.reading: Mateo started a podcast to review books from the school library. His first episodes were so long that many classmates stopped listening before the end. A teacher suggested making each episode shorter and ending with one question for listeners. After Mateo changed the format, more classmates sent comments and book ideas. He realized that good communication depends on the audience, not on saying everything you know.
questions.question: What was the problem with Mateo's first episodes?
questions.explanation: They were too long, so many classmates stopped listening before the end.

questions.reading: Mateo started a podcast to review books from the school library. His first episodes were so long that many classmates stopped listening before the end. A teacher suggested making each episode shorter and ending with one question for listeners. After Mateo changed the format, more classmates sent comments and book ideas. He realized that good communication depends on the audience, not on saying everything you know.
questions.question: What change did the teacher suggest?
questions.explanation: The teacher suggested shorter episodes with one question at the end.

questions.reading: Mateo started a podcast to review books from the school library. His first episodes were so long that many classmates stopped listening before the end. A teacher suggested making each episode shorter and ending with one question for listeners. After Mateo changed the format, more classmates sent comments and book ideas. He realized that good communication depends on the audience, not on saying everything you know.
questions.question: What did Mateo learn about communication?
questions.explanation: He learned to think about the audience and choose information carefully.
options: Book reviews should include every detail

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Neither of my parents is often treated as singular in careful English, so has is the best answer here.
options: Neither of my parents have seen it.
Neither of my parents has seen it.
Neither my parents has seen it.
Neither of parents have seen it.

questions.question: The sooner we start, _____ we will finish.
questions.explanation: The + comparative..., the + comparative... links two changes.

questions.question: Take your umbrella; _____, you might get wet.
questions.explanation: Otherwise means if you do not do that.

questions.question: The team showed great _____ during the emergency. (brave)
questions.explanation: Brave is an adjective. Bravery is the noun.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: No matter how hard it is we should keep trying
questions.explanation: No matter how introduces a concession: the difficulty does not change the decision.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: I regret not asking for help earlier
questions.explanation: Regret + not + verb-ing expresses regret about something you did not do.

questions.question: Which sentence sounds most natural?
questions.explanation: Have difficulty is followed by verb-ing: have difficulty concentrating.
options: I have difficulty to concentrate when it is noisy.
I have difficulty concentrating when it is noisy.
I have difficulty concentrate when it is noisy.
I have difficulty for concentrating when it is noisy.
